specific_mechanism: KCSC: Kalman Covariance-Tracked State Cache. Maintain a compressed latent state x̂_t ∈ R^d and a diagonal covariance P_t ∈ R^d_+ for the entire context. Each new token is projected to (y_t, R_t) observation; state and covariance update via standard Kalman recursion. Attention to past is replaced by reading x̂_t with uncertainty-weighted gating using P_t.
llm_application: KCSC (Kalman Covariance-Tracked State Cache): Replace the per-layer KV-cache with a Kalman-style filter state (x̂_t, P_t). Each new token embedding e_t produces an observation y_t = H_φ(e_t) with learned noise R_t = R_φ(e_t). State update: K_t = P_{t-1} H^T (H P_{t-1} H^T + R_t)^{-1}; x̂_t = F x̂_{t-1} + K_t(y_t - H F x̂_{t-1}); P_t = (I - K_t H) P_{t-1}. Output queries attend to x̂_t with per-dimension softmax gain scaled by 1/√(diag(P_t) + ε), so high-uncertainty dimensions are down-weighted.
novelty_claim: Explicit Kalman gain + covariance tracking as KV-cache replacement, with uncertainty-weighted output gating, is not standard. KOSS (arXiv 2512.16723) uses Kalman-inspired selection but doesn't expose explicit per-dim covariance to attention; KalmanFormer learns Kalman gain via transformer rather than using the filter as cache architecture.
motivation_justification: Kalman filter IS the closed-form Bayesian estimator. SSMs are linear recurrences but typically don't track P_t. The candidate transfers the full closed-form recursion with covariance.
content_words: bayesian recursive update gain prior posterior

source_side: bayesian recursive update gain prior posterior
